QF.id: XXVA Code of Conduct and Ethical Standards_9

question.text: When a research analyst has a material conflict of interest at the time of publication, they must:

answer.text: Disclose the material conflict of interest in the research report
Publish the report without any mention of the conflict
Transfer the coverage to another analyst even if that analyst has no expertise in the sector

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI Regulation 19(i)(c) requires disclosure of any other material conflict of interest at the time of publication. Disclosure is the remedy — not cancellation or concealment. **The Trap:** Publication with proper disclosure is the correct approach. Cancelling or transferring coverage deprives clients of the analyst's expertise. Publishing without disclosure violates regulations. **Cross-Exam Context:** Material conflict disclosure is tested across Series XV and III-A. The disclosure-as-remedy principle is universal in securities regulation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Check the conflict disclosure section of every report before distributing — if you're aware of a material conflict that isn't disclosed, flag it to compliance before distributing the report.
